title: Fixed Prices in Most Shops
description: Most shops, cafes, and malls in Bangalore have fixed prices. Bargaining is largely confined to flea markets and is often seen as out of place in regular retail and city stores.
dos: Check for posted prices before assuming negotiation is possible
Reserve bargaining for flea markets (e.g., Chickpet, Commercial Street stalls)
Politely ask about discounts on bulk purchases instead
donts: Don't try to haggle in malls, branded outlets or cafes
Don't assume every vendor expects negotiation
Don't be pushy when asking for a lower price

title: Liberal, Western-Friendly Dress Code
description: Bangalore's cosmopolitan, IT-driven culture means Western clothing — shorts, sleeveless tops, short skirts — is widely accepted in most public spaces, especially in tech hubs and pubs.
dos: Wear what feels comfortable in most urban neighborhoods
Save modest dressing for temples and traditional family settings
Follow office dress codes in corporate environments
donts: Don't assume the whole city is conservative — it varies sharply by neighborhood
Don't wear beachwear or loungewear into religious sites

title: Mixed Vegetarian & Non-Vegetarian Culture
description: Unlike Ahmedabad's strongly vegetarian culture, Bangalore has a large non-vegetarian-eating population, and restaurants, work lunches and social gatherings commonly default to mixed menus.
dos: Mention dietary preference upfront when ordering for a group
Look for the green/red dot system on packaged food and menus
Explore the city's strong South Indian vegetarian options — they exist alongside non-veg ones
donts: Don't assume a restaurant or host will default to vegetarian
Don't be surprised if non-veg is the default suggestion at work lunches

title: Punctuality Expected in Professional Settings
description: Bangalore's IT/startup culture places real weight on punctuality for meetings and deadlines, in contrast to the more relaxed 'IST' attitude common in many other Indian cities.
dos: Arrive on time or a few minutes early for work meetings
Proactively communicate if you're going to be late
donts: Don't assume meetings will start late like in more relaxed cities
Don't repeatedly show up late to standups or client calls

title: Active Pub & Nightlife Culture
description: Bangalore is often called India's pub capital, with a large bar, brewery and nightlife scene that is normal and socially accepted among young professionals — a sharp contrast to Gujarat's dry-state prohibition.
dos: Carry valid ID — most venues check age (legal drinking age is 21)
Drink responsibly and use cabs or rideshares at night
Check venue closing times — local regulations enforce an 11 PM-1 AM cutoff
donts: Don't expect the same scene if you later travel to dry states like Gujarat or Bihar
Don't assume every social outing involves alcohol — plenty of alcohol-free venues exist too
